title: Net_kernel and connection supervision — connect_node, nodes/0
purpose: net_kernel is the process that manages distribution connections. It provides connect_node/1 (establish connection to a node), disconnect_node/1 (tear down connection), and nodes/0 (list connected nodes). In beamr, net_kernel is implemented as BIFs backed by the ConnectionManager rather than as a BEAM process.
task: Implement net_kernel BIFs for connection management.

requirements.title: net_kernel:connect_node/1
requirements.spec: Connect to a named node. Resolves the name, establishes TCP connection, performs handshake.
acceptance: connect_node(NodeName) returns true on successful connection
Returns false if connection fails (name not resolved, handshake rejected)
Test: connect two nodes, verify nodes() lists both

requirements.title: erlang:nodes/0 and erlang:disconnect_node/1
requirements.spec: List connected nodes and disconnect from a specific node.
acceptance: nodes() returns list of connected node name atoms
disconnect_node(NodeName) closes the connection and returns true
After disconnect, nodes() no longer lists that node
Disconnection triggers noconnection exit signals (B-122 R3)
Test: connect, verify in nodes(), disconnect, verify removed

requirements.title: erlang:is_alive/0
requirements.spec: Returns true if the node is alive (distribution started), false otherwise.
acceptance: is_alive() returns false in non-distributed mode (nonode@nohost)
is_alive() returns true when distribution is configured with a real node name
Test: verify is_alive reflects distribution state

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement net_kernel as a BEAM process — implement as BIFs backed by ConnectionManager
SHALL NOT implement automatic reconnection — manual connect_node only
SHALL NOT implement hidden nodes or node types
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
